'''''The Rise and Fall of Darth Vader''''' is a young adult novel written by [[Ryder Windham]]. Scheduled for release in [[October]] [[2007]],<ref name="Literature">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/young/news20070515.html|text=The Saga from Vader's Point of View}}</ref> it was released early in [[September]] of the same year. It re-tells the story of all six ''[[Star Wars]]'' films from the point of view of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].

==Publisher's summary==
''This is the legendary story of Anakin Skywalker as it's never been told before - through his eyes…''

''From rise to fall, from light to dark, and back again.''

''Born a slave.''

''Raised a Jedi.''

''Feared by a Galaxy.''

==Plot summary==
The book begins with [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] [[Dream/Legends|dreaming]] for the first time in [[Standard year/Legends|years]] while aboard the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] shortly before it departs for the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]]. Vader thinks about his [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|son]] and his [[Palpatine/Legends|master]] and wonders if he is having a [[Force vision/Legends|premonition of the future]]. He dreams of the experiences he had as Anakin Skywalker, reminiscing about his life.

He and [[Shmi Skywalker Lars/Legends|Shmi Skywalker]] are aboard a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] being taken to [[Gardulla Besadii the Elder/Legends|Gardulla Besadii the Elder]] as [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]]. [[Watto/Legends|Watto]] sees Anakin busy repairing a [[Podracer/Legends|podracer]] and he says that he taught himself how to fix it. Watto is intrigued by this and takes Anakin and Shmi as payment for a lost bet.

The story continues with Anakin's life on Tatooine, his participation in the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]], his life as a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] and his motives for turning to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. Eventually, events end with his death during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] and his passing into the [[Netherworld of the Force/Legends|netherworld of the Force]].

Numerous passages from the book involving [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan]] would be lifted directly into ''[[The Life and Legend of Obi-Wan Kenobi]]'', a similar biography of the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] released the following year.

Although most of the book references the six films, there are references to other works of ''Star Wars'' literature. These references include:

*''[[Anakin's Fate]]''
*[[Star Wars Journal: Anakin Skywalker]]
*''[[Jedi Quest: Path to Truth]]''
*''[[Rogue Planet]]''
*''[[Jedi Quest: The Dangerous Games]]''
*''[[Jedi Quest: The Final Showdown]]''
*''[[Star Wars: Racer Revenge]]''
*''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]''
*[[Republic 71|''Republic'' 71]]
*''[[Star Wars: Obsession]]''
*''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]''
*[[Purge (comic book)|''Star Wars: Purge'']]
*{{Tales|15|First Impressions (comic story)}}
*[[Star Wars (radio)|''Star Wars: A New Hope Radio Drama'']]
*[[Star Wars: Vader's Quest|''Vader's Quest'']]
*''[[Planet of Kadril]]''
*''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]''
*''[[Darth Vader Strikes]]''
*''[[The Return of Ben Kenobi]]''
*''[[Race for Survival]]''
*''[[Splinter of the Mind's Eye]]''
*{{Tales|6|Thank the Maker}}
*[[Star Wars (1977) 61|''Star Wars'' (1977) 61]]
*[[Star Wars (1977) 63|''Star Wars'' (1977) 63]]
*''[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]''

==Release and reception==
In Japan, the book was entitled 「ダース・ヴェイダー 光と影」, translating to "Darth Vader: Light and Shadow".

==Continuity==
The first in a series of biography-type novels that would also spotlight [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]], this book dramatizes [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Vader's]] role in the films from his point of view, but also makes mention of several major stories from the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]], like ''[[Splinter of the Mind's Eye]]'' ([[1978]]). It includes new scenes, such as Anakin Skywalker's arrival on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] at age three.<ref name="TERC" />

In the first printing of this book, [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]] is misspelled "Mos Epsa" on page 19.
